intent:
Produce a Timeline / Evolution report tracing the evolution of MOOCs and online education from 2012 to 2026. The report is NOT a review; it is a chronological narrative + current-day analysis. The task must include the following:

- Shopping keywords: online education book, educational technology, tablet computer, virtual learning environment, educational apps, interactive learning devices, smart classroom technology, online tutoring, educational podcasts
- Reddit forums: technology, science, AskReddit, LifeProTips, personalfinance, politics, worldnews, education
- Wiki mandatory articles: Massive open online courses, History of distance education, Open educational resources, Learning management system, Flipped classroom, Online course platform, Open University, Coursera, EdX, FutureLearn

The report should be grounded in >= 120 sandbox URLs (>= 60 cited):

(A) `__SHOPPING__` >= 30 educational products and platforms.
(B) `__REDDIT__` >= 30 threads from specified forums.
(C) `__WIKIPEDIA__` >= 25 articles on relevant topics.

Format: markdown links only. Begin with the year 2012. No fabrication or chain-of-thought. Follow these synthesis requirements:

- Synthesis type: timeline
- Eras: 2012-2014, 2015-2017, 2018-2020, 2021-2026
- Minimum entries per era: 3 (wiki, shopping, reddit)
- Modern taxonomy of educational technology: structured list
- Debunked myths: 7, each cited with a wiki and reddit reference
- Explicitly forbid top 10 list generation
author_notes: Deep-tier task generated by gen_task_spec.py from configs/deep_topics/mooc_timeline.yaml. Golden: must=133, pool=581.
